section.para: This document is an example of how you can use MobilityDB in order to analyse data. It is directly inspired by the MobilityDB Workshop already existing. We will throughout this document present different use cases of MobilityDB, which is an extension on PostGIS and PostgreSQL.

This workshop was made by two students from INSA Rennes during an internship. We will use data from OpenStreetMap. The traces we are going to use in this example are rando_morbi1.gpx and rando_morbi2.gpx.
While this workshop illustrates the usage of MobilityDB functions, it does not explain them in detail. If you need help concerning the functions of MobilityDB, please refer to the documentation.

section.title: Importing the data
section.para: You can find in the repository for this workshop a CSV file called Freq_Mars2024 data.csv. It should look like the Figure 1.

figure.title: First few lines of the table
